new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage produced by blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, bearing the Scotch whisky origin quality.

new_classes.label_en: blended Scotch whisky in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2208 30 71)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Scotch Whisky in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 L (KN 2208 30 71)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended Scotch whisky, produced by blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, presented in containers with a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, as classified under CN subheading 2208 30 71.
A single malt Scotch whisky used as an ingredient in the production of blended Scotch whisky by blending.
A single grain Scotch whisky used as an ingredient in the production of blended Scotch whisky by blending.

coverage_explanation: The axioms cover all key distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 30 71: (1) the product is a blended Scotch whisky produced by the blending process, (2) it bears the Scotch whisky origin quality, (3) it is composed of single malt Scotch whisky and single grain Scotch whisky ingredients, and (4) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 L (via the SmallContainer restriction). A minor gap is that the legal note mentions 'one or more' of each ingredient type (cardinality ≥ 1), which is captured by someValuesFrom but OWL existential restrictions do not explicitly enforce the plurality ('one or more' vs 'at least one'). The container volume threshold (≤ 2 L) is captured by reusing the existing eucn:SmallContainer TBox class. The sibling code 22083079 (containers > 2 L) is not modelled here but is the complement at the parent level.
